name: Grilled Polenta with Fontina Cheese, Shiitake Mushrooms, and Sun-Dried Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Cup Shiitake Mushrooms, Dried and Canned, Minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring water, salt, pepper, and thyme to a boil in a large saucepan.
2. Slowly beat polenta with a whisk until smooth, avoiding lumps.
3. Reduce heat to low and stir continuously to prevent sticking. Cook for 10 minutes.
4. In a separate pan, sauté mushrooms and green onions in 2 tablespoons of butter until cooked through and lightly browned. Season with salt and pepper, add wine, and reduce until most of the wine has evaporated.
5. Add the mushroom mixture, remaining butter, and parsley to the polenta.
6. Spread the polenta mixture on a buttered cake pan or cookie sheet so that it is about 1/2 inch thick. Cool completely, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for up to one day.
7. Preheat grill over mesquite.
8. Remove polenta from refrigerator and cut into diamonds or other interesting shapes.
9. Grill the polenta until lightly toasted on the surface. Turn off heat, place a slice of Fontina cheese on top, and allow it to melt slightly.
10. Serve warm, garnished with grilled shiitake mushrooms and slivers of sun-dried tomatoes.
11. Serve with Ridge-Lytton Springs Zinfandel.
